releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Obsidian Edge TTS - Version 3.4.0 Release Notes

This release introduces a new reading feature and significantly improves reliability by adapting to **new requirements from the underlying Microsoft Edge text-to-speech service.**

#### ✨ New Features

* **Read from Cursor Aloud**: A new command and right-click menu option, "Read from cursor aloud," has been added. You can now start playback from your current cursor position to the end of the note. This is especially useful for listening to long documents section by section.

#### 🛠️ Changes & Improvements

* **Smarter Chunking for API Compliance**: The core logic for handling long notes has been completely reworked to align with new API rules.
    * Chunking is now based on a **fixed 4,096-byte limit** to perfectly match the new requirements of the text-to-speech service.
    * This change significantly improves the reliability of generating audio for very long notes and prevents errors.
    * The user setting for chunk size has been removed, as this size is now fixed for optimal performance.
* **Updated Content Limits**: To ensure stability, the maximum content length for a single playback or MP3 generation has been adjusted to **2,500 words** or **15,000 characters**. This is a proactive measure to work within new API constraints, including a 10-minute audio generation limit, to prevent timeouts.
* **Dependency Updates**: The underlying `edge-tts-universal` library has been updated to the latest version to incorporate these API changes.

#### A Note on API Changes & Reliability

Recently, the free Microsoft text-to-speech service that this plugin relies on introduced new API requirements, including a **10-minute audio limit** per request and other rate limits that are still being fully understood.

We have updated the plugin to navigate these changes, which is the primary reason for the reworked chunking system and new content limits. While we have worked around most of the issues, some lingering problems may still occur, particularly when streaming or generating audio for very long texts.

We appreciate your patience as we continue to monitor the service and refine the plugin's stability. Please be sure to make use of the new **"Read from cursor aloud"** feature, as it's a great way to work with longer notes under these new constraints. If you encounter other issues, please report them on GitHub or on our Discord.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/travisvn/obsidian-edge-tts/compare/3.3.2...3.4.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 🆕 Version 3.3.0 — Granular Filtering + Smarter Symbol Replacement This release introduces powerful new customization options to improve how your notes are processed for speech. ### 🎯 Major Features - **🧹 Granular Text Filtering Settings** - Fully control which elements are filtered before text-to-speech generation. - Options now include: frontmatter, markdown links, inline code, code blocks, HTML tags, tables, images, callouts, highlights, footnotes, comments, math expressions, and wiki links. - All settings are accessible via a new collapsible section in the plugin options. - **🔤 Smart Comparison Symbol Replacement** - Replace symbols like `<`, `>`, `<=`, and `>=` with written-out text. - Supports automatic language detection based on Obsidian’s UI language, with manual overrides and custom replacements also available. - Great for improving readability and avoiding XML issues in exported TTS output. ### 🛠️ Improvements & Refactors - Internal functions for filtering and symbol replacement have been overhauled for clarity and maintainability. - Comparison symbol translations have been moved to a dedicated module. --- If anyone reading this has any issues (or positive feedback too!), please reach out on [the Discord server](http://tts.travisvn.com/discord) --- ### 🖼️ Screenshots > 💡 Click on the images to enlarge
--- **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/travisvn/obsidian-edge-tts/compare/3.2.6...3.3.0

---

✅ Fixed drag-and-drop for UI elements in mobile

If anyone reading this has any issues (or, positive feedback too! 😇), please reach out on [the Discord server](http://tts.travisvn.com/discord) 

The mobile implementation has been challenging to test to the standard I usually try to hold to, given the constraints of Obsidian plugin development. So while it seems to work well from my haphazard tests, I still want to consider it in "beta" for now — pending word from you all. 

👍

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 🎉 Mobile Support Beta - Edge TTS Now Works Everywhere! 📱

After being a **desktop-only plugin** since day one, Edge TTS is finally making the leap to mobile! 🚀

## 🆕 What's New

**📱 Mobile Compatibility**
- ✅ **Audio playback** now works seamlessly on mobile devices
- ✅ **Voice selection** and speed controls
- ✅ **All core TTS features** powered by Microsoft Edge's neural voices

**🔧 Cross-Platform Improvements**
- Enhanced mobile-specific UI adaptations
- Improved error handling for mobile environments

## 📝 Current Limitations (Mobile)

While we're excited about mobile support, there are a few things to keep in mind:
- **MP3 file generation** remains desktop-only due to mobile file system limitations

*💡 Pro tip: You can still use our companion mobile app for generating MP3 files from your notes!*

## 🧪 Beta Notice

This is a **beta release** for mobile support! While we've tested extensively, mobile environments can be unpredictable. Please:
- 🐛 Report any issues you encounter
- 💬 Share feedback on the mobile experience  

## 🙏 Thank You

This has been a long-requested feature, and we're thrilled to finally bring Edge TTS to your mobile Obsidian setup. The journey from desktop-only to universal compatibility has been exciting!

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 🐛 Bug Fix Release v3.1.1

## 🔧 Fixed: Windows "Ghost File" Issue

This release resolves the persistent **ghost file problem** that Windows users were experiencing when generating MP3 files. 

### 🚀 What's Fixed

- **🪟 Windows Ghost Files**: MP3 files now appear correctly in both Obsidian and Windows File Manager
- **🛡️ Improved File Safety**: Enhanced file name sanitization removes forbidden characters (`\ / : * ? " < > |`) across all platforms  
- **🌐 Cross-Platform Compatibility**: Added `normalizePath()` for reliable path handling on all operating systems

### 🔍 Technical Details

The issue was caused by using low-level file system operations that bypassed Obsidian's internal cache system. We've switched to using Obsidian's proper vault API (`app.vault.createBinary()`) which maintains file system consistency and prevents cache synchronization issues.

### 👥 Who This Helps

- **Windows users** who experienced files showing in Obsidian but not in File Manager
- **All users** who had issues with special characters in file names
- **Cross-platform users** who experienced path-related inconsistencies

**Issues addressed:** https://github.com/travisvn/obsidian-edge-tts/issues/45, https://github.com/travisvn/obsidian-edge-tts/issues/50

---

**Full Changelog**: [3.1.0...3.1.1](https://github.com/travisvn/obsidian-edge-tts/compare/3.1.0...3.1.1)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 🎵 v3.1.0 - Generate Audio from Much Larger Notes! 

## 📖➡️🎧 Major Upgrade for Long-Form Content

Create MP3 narrations from **much larger notes** than ever before! This release dramatically expands what you can turn into audio narration. 🚀

### ✨ What's New

**📚 Expanded Content Support**
- Generate audio from notes up to **5,000 words** or **30,000 characters** (~30 minutes of audio narration)
- Intelligent chunked processing breaks large content into manageable pieces
- Smart content handling with boundary-aware truncation when limits are reached

**📊 Progress Tracking & Transparency**
- Beautiful visual progress indicator for large content processing
- Real-time chunk-by-chunk status updates
- Clear notifications about content processing and any truncation

**⚙️ Robust Processing**
- Configurable chunk size settings (5,000-15,000 characters)
- Enhanced error handling - partial failures don't stop the entire generation
- Force chunked generation command for manual control

### 🎯 Perfect For

- 📝 Long research notes and academic papers
- 📖 Book chapters and substantial writings
- 📋 Comprehensive documentation and guides
- 🗂️ Extensive meeting notes and reports

### 🎉 The Bottom Line

While there are still practical limits, this update **massively** expands what you can turn into audio. Previously challenging content now processes smoothly with intelligent chunking and beautiful progress feedback!

---

*Ready to narrate your longer notes? Update now and experience the improved capabilities!* ✨

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/travisvn/obsidian-edge-tts/compare/3.0.1...3.1.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 🚀 Edge TTS v3.0.0 - Major Feature Release

This is our biggest update yet! Version 3.0 introduces powerful new productivity features and enhanced playback controls that transform how you listen to your Obsidian notes.

## 🎵 **NEW: Playback Queue System**
Create uninterrupted listening sessions with our brand new queue functionality:
- **Queue multiple notes** for continuous playback - perfect for reviewing daily notes, research documents, or entire folders
- **Easy queue manager** with visual controls for reordering, removing, and managing your playlist
- **Loop functionality** - set your queue to repeat automatically for background listening
- **Auto-scroll** - the queue automatically highlights and scrolls to the currently playing item
- **Right-click integration** - easily add notes to your queue from the file explorer or editor

## 🎯 **Smart Auto-Pause**
Never lose your place again! The new auto-pause feature:
- **Automatically pauses** when you switch away from Obsidian
- **Resumes seamlessly** when you return to the app
- Perfect for multitasking workflows

## ⏱️ **Sleep Timer**
Wind down with confidence using the new sleep timer:
- Set timers for **15, 30, or 60 minutes**
- Automatically stops playback when the timer expires
- Great for bedtime listening or focused work sessions

## ⌨️ **Enhanced Keyboard Controls**
Navigate your audio like a pro with new hotkeys:
- **Ctrl+Space** - Pause/Resume (just like media players!)
- **Ctrl+← / Ctrl+→** - Jump backward/forward 10 seconds
- **Ctrl+Shift+R** - Read selected text aloud
- **Ctrl+Shift+Q** - Show queue manager

## 🎛️ **Advanced Playback Controls**
- **Jump forward/backward** - skip through content with 10-second increments
- **Enhanced floating player** - now shows queue status and streaming progress
- **Replay functionality** - easily restart the current audio
- **Improved seeking** - scrub through audio with precise timeline control

## ⚙️ **Granular Settings Control**
Take full control over your experience:
- **Toggle queue feature** on/off based on your workflow
- **Enable/disable auto-pause** on window focus loss
- **Customize floating controls** - hide them if you prefer minimal UI
- **Queue position memory** - your queue manager remembers where you placed it

## 🎮 **New Commands Available**
Access all features through the command palette:
- Add current note/selection to queue
- Play entire queue from beginning
- Clear playback queue
- Show queue status
- Set sleep timers (15/30/60 min)
- Toggle queue manager visibility

---

**Want to try the queue feature?** Right-click on any note and select "Add to playback queue", then use Ctrl+Shift+Q to open the queue manager!

## 🛠️ **Full Changelog**

### Added
- Complete playback queue system with visual queue manager
- Auto-pause on window focus loss setting
- Sleep timer functionality (15/30/60 minutes)
- Enhanced keyboard shortcuts for media control
- Jump forward/backward 10-second controls
- Background task monitoring with status bar progress
- Queue loop and auto-scroll features
- MediaSource Extensions for improved streaming

### Improved
- Floating player UI with queue status display
- Better audio seeking and timeline controls
- Enhanced settings organization with queue toggles
- Improved error handling and user feedback
- Memory management with automatic task cleanup

### Fixed
- Audio playback stability improvements
- Better handling of window resize events
- Improved position saving for UI elements

---

**Enjoying the new features?** Please ⭐ star the repository if this plugin helps your workflow!

> 💡 **Tip**: All queue features can be disabled in settings if you prefer the classic single-note playback experience.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/travisvn/obsidian-edge-tts/compare/2.1.0...3.0.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## ➡️ Fixed tabbed text filtering 

Text that began with tabs (including indented subsections) was being filtered before being sent to the API as part of the `filter out code blocks` portion of the markdown filter. 

This has been refined so that only code blocks are filtered 🤗 

Fixed issue: https://github.com/travisvn/obsidian-edge-tts/issues/35

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/travisvn/obsidian-edge-tts/compare/2.0.0...2.1.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Biggest update yet! 🥳

- Added floating playback controls! 
- Revamped logic holistically. We initially stream the incoming TTS API response until the entire stream has been retrieved and then we seamlessly switch over to playing the temporary mp3 file
- Options in the settings to turn any of this on / off
- A couple added commands related to the floating playback controls to help out

### Floating Playback Controls

When you start a narration, a floating player will appear, providing convenient control over your audio playback.

-   **Controls**: Play/pause, stop, jump backward/forward, and a seek bar to navigate through the audio.
-   **Close**: Click the 'X' icon on the player to close it.
-   **Streaming Indicator**: While the audio is streaming (before the full duration is known), the player will display "Streaming..." and the seek bar will be disabled. Once the full audio is loaded, the total duration will be shown. If the duration cannot be determined (e.g., for very long streams), an infinity symbol (∞) will be displayed.
-   **Draggable**: Click and drag any part of the player (except buttons and the seek bar) to move it around your workspace. Your preferred position is saved and restored for future sessions. The default position is the bottom-right of your screen. You can reset to this default using a command (see Usage).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Finally, support on mobile! 🎉 _(sorta)_ ## ✨ _New_ Mobile Option 📱 Create audio narration for your Obsidian notes through our free mobile app

- Available on **iOS** and **Android** - Usage: - Use the "share" functionality on any given note - Select `Read Aloud AI` from the native mobile share menu - Your markdown will be imported from the shared file and formatted like in the Obsidian desktop plugin - Narration will playback instantly, and you can export the `.mp3` generated back into Obsidian If you have any questions about the flow between Obsidian mobile and `Read Aloud AI` or any issues, feel free to stop by the [Discord](https://readaloudai.com/discord) **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/travisvn/obsidian-edge-tts/compare/1.10.0...1.11.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 🚀 Version 1.10.0 - Refactor & Background Processing

This update brings a major refactor to the plugin architecture, introducing better modularization, background task support, and UI improvements. 🎧✨

### 🔧 Refactoring & Architecture
- Modularized plugin code into separate managers for:
  - Audio playback 🎵
  - File operations 📂
  - Settings ⚙️
  - TTS engine 🗣️
  - UI components 🖥️
- Improved maintainability and performance with a cleaner structure.

### ⏳ Background Task Processing
- Implemented background task handling for TTS generation.
- Added progress tracking to keep things smooth and responsive.

### 🎛️ UI & Status Bar Improvements
- Enhanced the status bar with task progress indicators and better controls.
- Updated styles and added subtle animations for a more polished experience.

### 📌 Other Changes
- Bumped the minimum Obsidian version to **1.7.0** to support newer APIs.
- Updated date generation to respect the app’s language settings.


As always, feedback and contributions are welcome. 😊

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/travisvn/obsidian-edge-tts/compare/1.9.0...1.10.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Updates
- Reworked the markdown filtering so that mathematical comparisons <, >, <=, and >= should now be properly spoken
- Reworked the ampersand (&) issue to accommodate languages other than English
  - Previous setting is now an override to opt out of this (should rarely be set as "true" / "on")

Hopefully this new way of parsing does not hit any edge cases that I have not tested — please let me know in the Discord or elsewhere if that isn't the case. 

Happy New Year, everyone! 🥳


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/travisvn/obsidian-edge-tts/compare/1.6.0...1.7.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Updates
- Added filtering of ampersand (&) symbol 
  - Fixes bug in TTS that would otherwise not read aloud a note containing an ampersand 
- Added option to settings for how to handle its removal
  - Enabled: replace symbol with the word "and" — Disabled: remove symbol
  - Added so that non-English notes don't wind up having the word "and" thrown in

### Addressed issues:
- https://github.com/travisvn/obsidian-edge-tts/issues/18

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/travisvn/obsidian-edge-tts/compare/1.5.0...1.6.0
